steps.title: Recognize the scale factor rule for areas
steps.content: The side length of square A is 246 times the side length of square B, so the linear scale factor is 246. For any two similar figures, when linear dimensions scale by a factor $r$, the areas scale by $r^2$. Here, $r = 246$.

steps.title: Calculate the area scale factor and match the answer
steps.content:
The area of square A is $246^2$ times the area of square B, so $k = 246^2$.
Computing: $246^2 = 60{,}516$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
